title: Quiz: Coherence as a Quantum Resource
description: Test your understanding of the resource theory of quantum coherence

questions.question: In the resource theory of coherence, which states are considered 'free' (incoherent)?

options.text: States diagonal in the reference basis

questions.explanation: Incoherent (free) states are those diagonal in the chosen reference basis: δ = Σᵢ pᵢ|i⟩⟨i|. These have no off-diagonal elements and therefore no coherence.

questions.question: The l₁-norm of coherence for the state |+⟩ = (|0⟩ + |1⟩)/√2 is:
questions.explanation: For |+⟩⟨+| = [[1/2, 1/2], [1/2, 1/2]], the l₁-coherence is the sum of absolute values of off-diagonal elements: |1/2| + |1/2| = 1.

questions.question: Which class of incoherent operations is the LARGEST?
questions.explanation: The hierarchy is DIO ⊆ SIO ⊆ IO ⊆ MIO. MIO is the largest class, containing any operation that maps all incoherent states to incoherent states.

questions.question: The relative entropy of coherence equals:

options.text: S(ρ) - S(Δ(ρ)), where Δ is dephasing
S(Δ(ρ)) - S(ρ), where Δ is dephasing
The trace distance to the nearest incoherent state

questions.explanation: C_rel(ρ) = S(Δ(ρ)) - S(ρ), where Δ(ρ) is the completely dephased state. Since Δ(ρ) has higher or equal entropy, this quantity is non-negative.

questions.question: Coherence can be converted to entanglement using incoherent operations and an incoherent ancilla.
questions.explanation: True. A CNOT gate with a coherent control qubit and |0⟩ target creates entanglement proportional to the input coherence. This is the coherence-entanglement duality.

questions.question: What distinguishes coherence from entanglement as a quantum resource?

options.text: Coherence is defined relative to a fixed basis

questions.explanation: Unlike entanglement, coherence is basis-dependent. The same state can be coherent in one basis (e.g., |+⟩ in computational basis) and incoherent in another (e.g., |+⟩ in the X basis where it's an eigenstate).

questions.question: The distillable coherence C_d(ρ) in the asymptotic limit equals:
questions.explanation: In the asymptotic limit with incoherent operations, the distillable coherence equals the relative entropy of coherence: C_d(ρ) = C_rel(ρ) = S(Δ(ρ)) - S(ρ).

questions.question: Which statements about coherence measures are correct? (Select all that apply)

options.text: l₁-coherence is easy to compute (closed form)
All coherence measures agree on the ordering of states
The relative entropy of coherence has operational meaning as distillable coherence
Coherence measures are monotonically non-increasing under incoherent operations

questions.explanation: (a) Correct: C_l1 = Σᵢ≠ⱼ|ρᵢⱼ| is easily computed. (b) Incorrect: Different measures can give different orderings. (c) Correct: C_rel equals distillable coherence asymptotically. (d) Correct: This is the defining property of coherence monotones.
